turns.speaker: ONCOLOGIST
turns.text: Let's discuss Jordan Rivera, 69, newly diagnosed stage IIIA NSCLC adenocarcinoma, T3 N1 M0.

turns.speaker: PATHOLOGIST
turns.text: Adenocarcinoma confirmed. Molecular panel back: PD-L1 is 70 percent, ALK negative.

turns.speaker: ONCOLOGIST
turns.text: Given the high PD-L1 I'd lean toward concurrent chemoradiation followed by immunotherapy consolidation.

turns.speaker: RADIOLOGIST
turns.text: Imaging supports N1 nodal involvement, no distant disease. Suitable for radiation planning.

turns.speaker: SURGEON
turns.text: This is potentially resectable. I'd put a lobectomy on the table as the definitive option.

turns.speaker: ONCOLOGIST
turns.text: Okay, let's go with the radiotherapy-based approach over surgery. Radiation oncology to plan this week.

turns.speaker: NURSE_COORDINATOR
turns.text: I'll schedule the radiation planning CT and set up the immunotherapy consult.

turns.speaker: ONCOLOGIST
turns.text: Good. I think that's our plan. Anything else? No? Let's move to the next case.
